mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in quarterly GDP growth signals strong economic demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% YoY despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 245 compresses real interest rates, prompting equity investors to bid up valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 245 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal aggressive monetary tightening amid inflation, which raises discount rates and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exceed thresholds despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production below 104.6 indicates supply-side disruptions, which reduce corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 104.6 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth above 147,010 thousand persons signals robust labor market conditions, which support consumer spending and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 147,010 despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate below 4% indicates tight labor market conditions, which may lead to wage inflation and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in residential construction and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate strong demand conditions, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST rise despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate above 1.15% signals monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.15 despite negative signal.
